commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=aa687112e957b771f783cee8edaf9891b3ffcfe2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Docker bind host roots to Codex app-server workspace-write policy, gates native Code Mode for Docker alias/shadow bind layouts, exports sandbox bind helpers, updates tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main sends only the effective cwd as Codex `writableRoots`, while docs say writable Docker binds remain independent of `workspaceAccess`. I did not run a live Docker/Codex turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs human/author follow-up because the current patch has a P1 tool-routing blocker and the real-behavior proof does not demonstrate the final agent write path.

target.title: fix(codex): honor Docker bind write policy
target.body:
Summary:
- mirror writable Docker bind host roots into Codex app-server workspace-write policy when the bind can be represented safely on the host
- disable Codex native Code Mode for Docker bind aliases such as `host:/data:rw`, and for writable bind roots that contain read-only child bind shadows
- add focused regression coverage for writable bind roots, alias detection, read-only shadow detection, and Codex native tool gating

Fixes #83737

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (3 files, 204 tests passed)
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean after fixing the accepted read-only child bind finding)
- AWS Crabbox `run_bca4d3cca6c6`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_77513bfe085a`, exit 0

Real behavior proof:
Behavior addressed: Docker sandbox with `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and external writable bind `host:/data:rw` must allow writes through OpenClaw's Docker-backed sandbox path without letting Codex host-native tools misrepresent container-only paths.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox direct provider, `run_bca4d3cca6c6`, lease `cbx_77513bfe085a`, Linux target with Docker installed during the proof script.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Full-resynced the patched checkout to AWS, installed Node 24/pnpm/Docker, ran `pnpm build`, created a real Docker sandbox using `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `${bindRoot}:/data:rw`, wrote `patched` via `sandbox.backend.runShellCommand`, then ran `runCodexAppServerAttempt` against a protocol-shaped fake app-server client to inspect the actual `thread/start` and `turn/start` payloads from this code path.
Evidence after fix: Proof JSON reported `dockerWriteStdout: "patched"`, `hostProof: "patched"`, `nativeCodeMode: false`, `nativeEnvironments: []`, and `turnWritableRoots` containing both the sandbox workspace and external bind host root.
Observed result after fix: Docker-backed `/data` write succeeded and Codex app-server native Code Mode was disabled for the container-path alias, so host-native tools no longer try to write `/data` outside the Docker namespace.
What was not tested: A real OpenAI-authenticated Codex app-server/model turn was not run; the proof used a protocol-shaped fake app-server client so no live credentials were required.
target.author: joshavant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
